AdbcTransport.description: ADBC (Arrow Database Connectivity) database transport contract. Spec: §Transport Contracts.

db_kwargs.description: Driver-specific keyword arguments passed to the ADBC driver on connect (e.g. Snowflake account/warehouse, Postgres options). Values may be literals or value expressions (`{"ref": "..."}`, `{"template": "..."}`, `{"function": "..."}`); the pipeline runtime resolves them before invoking the driver. Note: schema introspection (`list_schemas`, `test_credentials`, …) takes a separate path — it constructs driver kwargs from canonical credential fields (`account`, `warehouse`, `username`, …) and does NOT consume connector-authored `db_kwargs`. This matches the SQLAlchemy introspection path, which ignores the connector DSN template and rebuilds the URI from credential fields. Optional individually, but `AdbcTransport` requires at least one of `dsn` / `db_kwargs`.
db_kwargs.title: Db Kwargs

driver.description: ADBC driver family identifier. Closed to the drivers the engine actually ships. Required at the connector layer (unlike `SqlAlchemyTransport.driver`); the ADBC dispatcher selects the matching driver and cannot fall back. Not to be confused with the format-dialect inputs on file/s3 connectors.
driver.title: Driver

dsn.description: Structured URL-template DSN with bindings and encodings. Connector-authored layout; the pipeline runtime renders and substitutes bindings before invoking the ADBC driver. Note: schema introspection takes a separate path — it rebuilds the URI from canonical credential fields and does NOT consume the connector-authored DSN template (symmetric to the SQLAlchemy introspection path). Optional individually, but `AdbcTransport` requires at least one of `dsn` / `db_kwargs`; ADBC drivers that accept all connection state via `db_kwargs` (e.g. Snowflake) may omit `dsn`.

AdbcTransport.title: AdbcTransport

ApiConnector.description: API-type connector. Provider configuration lives on `transports.<name>` (HttpTransport).

$schema.description: Connector schema URL (optional in API payloads, required for standalone files). Accepts the published URL on any environment host (schemas.analitiq.<tld>) so a document authored against the canonical analitiq.ai URL validates against the per-environment schema the engine fetches at runtime.
$schema.title: $Schema

connection_contract.description: Connector-level connection contract: declared inputs, post-auth outputs, activation requirements, and cross-input validation.

connector_id.description: Connector's canonical identifier and registry repo name (e.g. `postgres`, `xero`, `pipedrive`). Must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores, starting with a letter or a digit.
connector_id.title: Connector Id

default_transport.description: Name of the entry in `transports` used when an operation omits `transport_ref`.
default_transport.title: Default Transport

display_name.description: User-facing connector label (1-120 chars, no leading/trailing whitespace).
display_name.title: Display Name

documentation_url.description: URI string pointing at the upstream provider or spec documentation for this connector. Connector-only metadata. Must be an absolute http(s) URL of ≤2048 characters when present.
documentation_url.title: Documentation Url

resource_discovery.description: Resource discovery declarations for dynamic or post-auth resources.

tags.description: Grouping/search labels (max 50 unique trimmed strings of 1-64 chars).
tags.title: Tags

transport_defaults.description: Defaults merged into every entry of `transports` (object-valued fields like `headers` deep-merge; scalars override per-entry).

transports.$comment: Each transport entry must declare its own `transport_type`. The Pydantic runtime accepts entries that omit it and inherits the value from `transport_defaults.transport_type`, but this JSON Schema does not — declare `transport_type` per entry for portable validation.
transports.description: Named transport contracts discriminated by `transport_type` (http | sqlalchemy | adbc | s3 | file | stdout). Each entry inherits `transport_defaults` and supplies type-specific fields.
transports.title: Transports

version.description: Connector release semantic version. Saved connections record this version for drift detection.
version.title: Version

write_unit.description: Preferred write-batch coalescing unit for this destination (issue #87). Connector-level because it is not a SQL-only fact: any destination whose write cost is per-write-operation may declare the batch size the engine's coalescer should target. Absent means no coalescing preference.

ApiConnector.title: ApiConnector

ApiKeyAuth.description: API key authentication. Required values declared in `connection_contract.inputs`.
ApiKeyAuth.title: ApiKeyAuth

AuthOperationTemplate.description:
Operation template for auth `authorize` / `token_exchange` / `refresh` / `test`.

The HTTP `base_url` lives on the named transport; this template selects the
transport via `transport_ref` (omit to use `default_transport`) and supplies
the per-operation `path`, headers, and body.

body.description: Request body; type depends on transport/encoding
body.title: Body

headers.description: Request headers; values may be literals, `{ref}`, `{template}`, or `{function}`
headers.title: Headers

headers_remove.description: Header names to delete from inherited transport defaults
headers_remove.title: Headers Remove

method.description: HTTP method. Engines treat absence as GET; declare it explicitly for non-HTTP transports.
method.title: Method

path.description: Path or relative URL on the selected transport
path.title: Path

transport_ref.description: Named transport this request is dispatched through; defaults to `default_transport`
transport_ref.title: Transport Ref

AuthOperationTemplate.title: AuthOperationTemplate

AwsIamAuth.description:
AWS IAM / role / profile / credential-chain auth.

Spec: §Authentication — must not declare OAuth-specific children. User
values (account, role, profile, …) live in `connection_contract.inputs`.
AwsIamAuth.title: AwsIamAuth

Base64EncodeDerived.description: `base64_encode` — base64-encode a string or bytes value.
Base64EncodeDerived.title: Base64EncodeDerived

BasicAuth.description:
Basic (username/password) authentication.

Required user-entered values are declared in `connection_contract.inputs`
(typically `username` and `password`). The connector should not declare
field names here.
BasicAuth.title: BasicAuth

BasicAuthDerived.description: `basic_auth` — build a Basic auth credential from username/password.
BasicAuthDerived.title: BasicAuthDerived

ConnectionConditionPredicate.description:
A single connection-input test used in
`connection_contract.validation.rules[].when`: declares `field` and exactly
one operator key (`eq`/`in`/`not_in`/`present`/`regex`).

eq.description: Scalar value the field must equal.
eq.title: Eq

field.description: Connection input key or saved connection field path the predicate tests.
field.title: Field

in.description: Non-empty array of allowed scalar values.
in.title: In

not_in.description: Non-empty array of disallowed scalar values.
not_in.title: Not In

present.description: Boolean literal true; the field must resolve to a non-empty value.
present.title: Present

regex.description: Regular expression matched against the resolved string value.
regex.title: Regex

ConnectionConditionPredicate.title: ConnectionConditionPredicate

ConnectionContract.description:
Connector-level contract for what a saved connection must contribute.

Source of truth for connection form rendering, save-time validation, drift
detection, and template reference validation. Spec: §Connection Contract.

No standalone `version` field — drift detection rides on `connector_version`
semver: patch = no shape change, minor = additive shape change, major =
breaking shape change.

inputs.description: Declared submitted/provisioned inputs keyed by their runtime `name`
inputs.title: Inputs

post_auth_outputs.description: Declared post-auth outputs keyed by their runtime `name`
post_auth_outputs.title: Post Auth Outputs

required_for_activation.description: Runtime reference paths that must resolve before the connection can be marked active (e.g. 'connection.parameters.host', 'secrets.password')
required_for_activation.title: Required For Activation

validation.description: Cross-input validation block; per-input rules belong on the input

ConnectionContract.title: ConnectionContract

ConnectionContractInput.description:
One submitted/provisioned value declared by the connection contract.

Spec: §Connection Inputs. The combination of `name` and `storage` determines
the runtime reference path (e.g. `connection.parameters.host`,
`secrets.api_key`).

enum.description: Authoritative allowed-value list for scalar inputs (non-empty when present).
enum.title: Enum

phase.description: Lifecycle phase the value is provisioned in (closed enum: pre_auth, auth).

required.description: Whether resolution must produce a value
required.title: Required

secret.description: Required when `storage` is `secrets`; must be `true` iff `storage` is `secrets`. Otherwise omitted or `false`.
secret.title: Secret

source.description: How the value is provisioned (closed enum: user, platform).

storage.description: Where the resolved value is durably stored (closed enum: connection.parameters, secrets).
storage.title: Storage

type.description: JSON value type used for validation and coercion. Spec: §Connection Inputs — closed vocabulary.
type.title: Type

ConnectionContractInput.title: ConnectionContractInput

ConnectionContractInputUI.description:
UI hints for a connection contract input. Spec: §Connection Inputs.

The spec calls these "Label, help text, widget, defaults, validation hints".
`default` here is the displayed-default-in-the-form value (which may differ
from the contract-resolution default on `ConnectionContractInput.default`).

default.description: Default value to pre-fill in the form widget
default.title: Default

help_text.description: Inline help text shown to the user
help_text.title: Help Text

options.description: Options for select-style widgets. When both `options` and the input's `enum` are provided they must enumerate the same value set — `ui.options` may not omit any `enum` value or add a value not in `enum` (spec: §Connection Inputs).
options.title: Options

placeholder.description: Placeholder text shown in empty inputs
placeholder.title: Placeholder

widget.description: Widget hint (text, password, select, textarea, number, ...)
widget.title: Widget

ConnectionContractInputUI.title: ConnectionContractInputUI

rules.description: Cross-input validation rules; per-input rules belong on the input itself
rules.title: Rules

ConnectionContractValidationRule.description: Cross-input declarative validation rule. Spec: §Cross-Input Validation.

when.description: Predicate that decides whether the rule applies

ConnectionContractValidationRule.title: ConnectionContractValidationRule

CredentialsAuth.description: Provider-specific credentials bundle (use only when no narrower type fits).
CredentialsAuth.title: CredentialsAuth

Accepts the published URL on any environment host (schemas.analitiq.<tld>) so a document authored against the canonical analitiq.ai URL validates against the per-environment schema the engine fetches at runtime.

connection_contract.description: Connector-level connection contract: declared inputs, post-auth outputs, activation requirements, and cross-input validation.

connector_id.description: Connector's canonical identifier and registry repo name (e.g. `postgres`, `xero`, `pipedrive`). Must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores, starting with a letter or a digit.
connector_id.title: Connector Id

default_transport.description: Name of the entry in `transports` used when an operation omits `transport_ref`.
default_transport.title: Default Transport

display_name.description: User-facing connector label (1-120 chars, no leading/trailing whitespace).
display_name.title: Display Name

documentation_url.description: URI string pointing at the upstream provider or spec documentation for this connector. Connector-only metadata. Must be an absolute http(s) URL of ≤2048 characters when present.
documentation_url.title: Documentation Url

resource_discovery.description: Resource discovery declarations for dynamic or post-auth resources.

sql_capabilities.description: Declared SQL write-path capabilities (engine ADR §5). SQL-specific — not present on other connector kinds. Optional; when omitted the engine refuses any needed-but-undeclared fact at handshake time. When present, all five facts are required.

tags.description: Grouping/search labels (max 50 unique trimmed strings of 1-64 chars).
tags.title: Tags

transport_defaults.description: Defaults merged into every entry of `transports` (object-valued fields like `headers` deep-merge; scalars override per-entry).

transports.$comment: Each transport entry must declare its own `transport_type`. The Pydantic runtime accepts entries that omit it and inherits the value from `transport_defaults.transport_type`, but this JSON Schema does not — declare `transport_type` per entry for portable validation.
transports.description: Named database transport contracts (`sqlalchemy` | `adbc`), discriminated by `transport_type`.
transports.title: Transports

version.description: Connector release semantic version. Saved connections record this version for drift detection.
version.title: Version

write_unit.description: Preferred write-batch coalescing unit for this destination (issue #87). Connector-level because it is not a SQL-only fact: any destination whose write cost is per-write-operation may declare the batch size the engine's coalescer should target. Absent means no coalescing preference.

DatabaseConnector.title: DatabaseConnector

DatabaseTls.description:
Database transport TLS declaration. Spec: §Transport Contracts.

Both fields resolve to plain strings at runtime. The interpretation of
those strings (libpq vocabulary, MySQL vocabulary, etc.) is owned by the
connector package's dialect — the schema is vocabulary-agnostic. No
canonical mode set is enforced here; the connector's
``connection_contract.inputs[<field>].enum`` is the user-facing constraint.

ca_certificate.description: CA certificate value-expression. Resolves to a plain string at runtime; required when `mode` implies certificate verification.
ca_certificate.title: Ca Certificate

mode.description: TLS mode value-expression. Resolves to a plain string at runtime; interpretation (e.g. libpq `verify-full` vs MySQL `REQUIRED`) is owned by the connector package's dialect.
mode.title: Mode

DatabaseTls.title: DatabaseTls

DbAuth.description: Database connection authentication. Inputs declared in `connection_contract.inputs`.
DbAuth.title: DbAuth

encoding.description: Generic encoding applied before substitution into the template

value.title: Value

Absent means no coalescing preference.

dialect.description: Format-specific dialect options as a value-expression bundle. Schema is `format`-dependent — see spec §Output Format.
dialect.title: Dialect

format.description: Output format value-expression. Closed value-level enum: `csv` | `jsonl`.
format.title: Format

path.description: Target output path. May embed `{stream_alias}` / `{run_id}` / `{date}` placeholders.
path.title: Path

base_url.description: Base URL: a non-empty literal string, or a value-expression (`{template}`/`{ref}`/`{literal}`/function) resolving to one at connection-materialization time (e.g. a per-tenant host taken from `connection.parameters` or discovered post-auth via `connection.discovered`). May be omitted when this entry exists only to extend `transport_defaults`.
base_url.title: Base Url

headers.description: Default request headers; values may be literals or expressions
headers.title: Headers

headers_remove.description: Header names to delete from inherited defaults (case-insensitive)
headers_remove.title: Headers Remove

InputPhase.description:
Lifecycle phase a connection contract input is provisioned in.

Closed enum: `pre_auth` or `auth`. Values produced after authentication
belong in `post_auth_outputs`.
InputPhase.title: InputPhase

InputSource.description:
How a connection contract input is provisioned.

Closed enum: `user` or `platform`. Values produced after authentication
belong in `post_auth_outputs`, not in `inputs` with a post-auth source.
InputSource.title: InputSource

JwtAuth.description: JWT-based authentication. Signing inputs are declared in `connection_contract.inputs`.
JwtAuth.title: JwtAuth

LiteralStringExpression.description:
`{literal}` form constrained to a non-empty string — the shape a
URL-valued field accepts (a general `{literal}` may wrap any value, but a
URL must be a string).
LiteralStringExpression.title: LiteralStringExpression

LookupDerived.description: `lookup` — map an input value through a connector-declared inline map.

map.description: Inline value map: input-value → mapped-output JSON value
map.title: Map

LookupDerived.title: LookupDerived

NoneAuth.description:
Marker for connectors that need no authentication workflow.

Spec: §Authentication — must not declare `authorize`, `token_exchange`, or
`refresh`. `test` remains optional per the shared `Auth object fields`
table.
NoneAuth.title: NoneAuth

OAuth2AuthorizationCodeAuth.description:
OAuth2 authorization code flow.

Spec: §Authentication — requires `authorize` and `token_exchange`; `refresh`
optional.
OAuth2AuthorizationCodeAuth.title: OAuth2AuthorizationCodeAuth

OAuth2ClientCredentialsAuth.description:
OAuth2 client credentials flow.

Spec: §Authentication — requires `token_exchange`; must omit `authorize`.
OAuth2ClientCredentialsAuth.title: OAuth2ClientCredentialsAuth

PostAuthOperationRequest.description:
Request template used by `options_request` / `discovery_request` to populate
a post-auth output. Spec: §Post-Auth Outputs.

body.description: Request body; type depends on transport/encoding
body.title: Body

headers.description: Request headers; values may be literals, `{ref}`, `{template}`, or `{function}`
headers.title: Headers

method.description: HTTP method. Engines treat absence as GET; declare it explicitly to signal the intent to non-HTTP transports.
method.title: Method

path.description: Path or relative URL on the selected transport
path.title: Path

transport_ref.description: Named transport this request is dispatched through; defaults to `default_transport`
transport_ref.title: Transport Ref

PostAuthOperationRequest.title: PostAuthOperationRequest

PostAuthOutput.description:
Durable post-auth output produced after authentication. Spec: §Post-Auth Outputs.

The post-auth output field set is closed except for `x-*` extension
metadata: `mode`, `storage`, `type`, `format`, `ui`, `options_request`,
`options_path`, `discovery_request`, `value_path`, `label_path`. `source`,
`phase`, `required`, and `secret` are explicitly NOT valid post-auth
output fields — `source`/`phase` are inherent to the enclosing
`post_auth_outputs` map; activation enforcement lives on
`required_for_activation`; secrecy is determined by `storage`.

discovery_request.description: Request that returns the value to auto-discover. Required for `auto_discovery`; forbidden for `user_selection`.

label_path.description: Response path used as the option label. Optional for `user_selection`; forbidden for `auto_discovery`.
label_path.title: Label Path

options_path.description: Response body path to the option array. Defaults to the response body root. Optional for `user_selection`; forbidden for `auto_discovery`.
options_path.title: Options Path

options_request.description: Request that returns selectable options. Required for `user_selection`; forbidden for `auto_discovery`.

storage.description: Closed enum. Must be `connection.selections` for `user_selection`, or `connection.discovered`/`secrets` for `auto_discovery`.
storage.title: Storage

type.description: JSON value type used for validation and coercion. Spec: §Post-Auth Outputs — same closed vocabulary as input `type`.
type.title: Type

value_path.description: Response path used to extract the stored value
value_path.title: Value Path

PostAuthOutput.title: PostAuthOutput

PostAuthOutputMode.description: How a post-auth output's value is produced. Spec: §Post-Auth Outputs.
PostAuthOutputMode.title: PostAuthOutputMode

RefExpression.description: `{ref}` form: a dotted path into the resolution context.
RefExpression.title: RefExpression

ResourceDiscovery.description:
Declarative resource discovery for connection-scoped private endpoints.

Spec: §Resource Discovery. Produces connection-scoped endpoints and type
maps under `connection.endpoints` / `connection.type_map`.

implementation.description: Strategy implementation source; omit to use a builtin strategy

transport_ref.description: Named transport used for discovery; defaults to `default_transport`
transport_ref.title: Transport Ref

ResourceDiscovery.title: ResourceDiscovery

ResourceDiscoveryImplementation.description: Discovery strategy implementation source. Spec: §Resource Discovery.

entrypoint.description: Plugin entrypoint string (e.g. `analitiq_acme.discovery:AcmeCatalogDiscovery`). Required when type=connector_plugin; must be omitted when type=builtin.
entrypoint.title: Entrypoint

ResourceDiscoveryImplementation.title: ResourceDiscoveryImplementation

tags.description: Grouping/search labels (max 50 unique trimmed strings of 1-64 chars).
tags.title: Tags

transport_defaults.description: Defaults merged into every entry of `transports` (object-valued fields like `headers` deep-merge; scalars override per-entry).

transports.$comment: Each transport entry must declare its own `transport_type`. The Pydantic runtime accepts entries that omit it and inherits the value from `transport_defaults.transport_type`, but this JSON Schema does not — declare `transport_type` per entry for portable validation.
transports.title: Transports

version.description: Connector release semantic version. Saved connections record this version for drift detection.
version.title: Version

write_unit.description: Preferred write-batch coalescing unit for this destination (issue #87). Connector-level because it is not a SQL-only fact: any destination whose write cost is per-write-operation may declare the batch size the engine's coalescer should target. Absent means no coalescing preference.

S3Connector.title: S3Connector

S3CredentialsBlock.description: Static S3 credentials block (access key + secret access key). Spec: §Transport Contracts.
S3CredentialsBlock.title: S3CredentialsBlock

credentials.description: Static AWS credentials; required for the v1 contract.

dialect.description: Format-specific dialect options as a value-expression bundle. Schema is `format`-dependent — see spec §Output Format.
dialect.title: Dialect

format.description: Output format value-expression. Closed value-level enum: `csv` | `jsonl`.
format.title: Format

prefix.description: Object key prefix or template value-expression. May embed `{stream_alias}` / `{run_id}` / `{date}` placeholders.
prefix.title: Prefix

SqlAlchemyTransport.description: SQLAlchemy database transport contract. Spec: §Transport Contracts.

driver.description: SQLAlchemy driver in `dialect+driver` form (e.g. `postgresql+asyncpg`, `mysql+aiomysql`, `redshift+redshift_connector`). May name a sync or an async DBAPI — no driver allow-list is imposed. The named driver must be a real SQLAlchemy dialect registration; that is checked at transport build, not here. Optional — SQLAlchemy can derive the driver from the DSN scheme — but declare it so the sync/async choice is explicit to a reader.
driver.title: Driver

dsn.description: Structured URL-template DSN with bindings and encodings.

tls.description: Generic TLS declaration; runtime materializes driver-specific args.

SqlAlchemyTransport.title: SqlAlchemyTransport

SqlCapabilities.description:
SQL write-path capabilities declared by a database connector (ADR §5).

"Refuse, don't guess": the engine reads these facts instead of probing the
live database, and refuses at handshake time when a needed fact was not
declared (analitiq-engine#390, PR analitiq-engine#400). Optional as a block,
but when present all five facts are required — a partial declaration is a
config error.

bulk_load.description: The bulk-ingest path the destination supports: `none`, Postgres `copy_from` (`COPY FROM`), MySQL `load_data_local_infile` (`LOAD DATA LOCAL INFILE`), ADBC bulk `adbc_ingest`, or BigQuery-style `load_job`.
bulk_load.title: Bulk Load

catalog.description: Catalog (multi-database) support: `none` — no catalog concept; `read` — the catalog is addressable but not creatable; `full` — the engine may create/drop catalogs.
catalog.title: Catalog

merge_form.description: The upsert grammar the destination supports: SQL-standard `merge` (`MERGE`), Postgres-style `insert_on_conflict` (`INSERT … ON CONFLICT`), MySQL-style `insert_on_duplicate_key` (`INSERT … ON DUPLICATE KEY`), or `none` (no native upsert).
merge_form.title: Merge Form

session_targeting.description: How the write target schema/catalog is selected: `per_statement` — fully qualified on each statement; `session_default` — set once as a session default (e.g. `search_path` / `USE`) and inherited.
session_targeting.title: Session Targeting

stage.description: Staging-relation capabilities for the merge/upsert write path.

SqlCapabilities.title: SqlCapabilities

SqlStageCapabilities.description:
Staging-relation capabilities for a SQL destination's write path (ADR §5).

Describes how the engine may materialize an intermediate stage relation
before merging into the target. `scope`, `schema`, and `transactional_ddl`
are always required; `dedicated_schema` is required iff `schema` is
`dedicated`, and must be omitted or null otherwise
(`_dedicated_schema_matches_scope`).

dedicated_schema.description: Name of the dedicated staging schema — the engine emits it as a SQL identifier, so it must be a non-blank name with no leading/trailing whitespace. Required when `schema` is `dedicated`; must be omitted or null otherwise.
dedicated_schema.title: Dedicated Schema

schema.description: Where stage relations live: `target` co-locates them in the target's own schema; `dedicated` places them in a separate schema named by `dedicated_schema`.
schema.title: Schema

scope.description: Stage-relation scope: `temp` for a session/transaction-scoped temporary table, `real` for an ordinary persistent table the engine creates and drops around the write.
scope.title: Scope

transactional_ddl.description: Whether the destination runs stage DDL (CREATE/DROP) inside the write transaction. `false` for engines that auto-commit DDL (e.g. MySQL), which forces a non-transactional staging strategy.
transactional_ddl.title: Transactional Ddl

SqlStageCapabilities.title: SqlStageCapabilities

StdoutConnector.description: Stdout connector — debug/print destination. `StdoutTransport` is a marker.

$schema.description: Connector schema URL (optional in API payloads, required for standalone files). Accepts the published URL on any environment host (schemas.analitiq.<tld>) so a document authored against the canonical analitiq.ai URL validates against the per-environment schema the engine fetches at runtime.
$schema.title: $Schema

auth.description: Stdout connectors carry no auth workflow (`type: 'none'`).

connection_contract.description: Connector-level connection contract: declared inputs, post-auth outputs, activation requirements, and cross-input validation.

connector_id.description: Connector's canonical identifier and registry repo name (e.g. `postgres`, `xero`, `pipedrive`). Must be lowercase alphanumeric with hyphens/underscores, starting with a letter or a digit.
connector_id.title: Connector Id

default_transport.description: Name of the entry in `transports` used when an operation omits `transport_ref`.
default_transport.title: Default Transport

display_name.description: User-facing connector label (1-120 chars, no leading/trailing whitespace).
display_name.title: Display Name

documentation_url.description: URI string pointing at the upstream provider or spec documentation for this connector. Connector-only metadata. Must be an absolute http(s) URL of ≤2048 characters when present.
documentation_url.title: Documentation Url

resource_discovery.description: Resource discovery declarations for dynamic or post-auth resources.

tags.description: Grouping/search labels (max 50 unique trimmed strings of 1-64 chars).
tags.title: Tags

transport_defaults.description: Defaults merged into every entry of `transports` (object-valued fields like `headers` deep-merge; scalars override per-entry).

transports.$comment: Each transport entry must declare its own `transport_type`. The Pydantic runtime accepts entries that omit it and inherits the value from `transport_defaults.transport_type`, but this JSON Schema does not — declare `transport_type` per entry for portable validation.
transports.title: Transports

version.description: Connector release semantic version. Saved connections record this version for drift detection.
version.title: Version

write_unit.description: Preferred write-batch coalescing unit for this destination (issue #87). Connector-level because it is not a SQL-only fact: any destination whose write cost is per-write-operation may declare the batch size the engine's coalescer should target. Absent means no coalescing preference.

StdoutConnector.title: StdoutConnector

format.description: Output format value-expression. Closed value-level enum: `csv` | `jsonl`. Default `jsonl`.
format.title: Format

TemplateExpression.description: `{template}` form: a `${scope.path}`-bearing string resolved at runtime.
TemplateExpression.title: TemplateExpression

TransportDefaults.description: Defaults merged into every entry of `transports`. Spec: §Transport Contracts.

headers.description: Default request headers shared across HTTP transport entries.
headers.title: Headers

headers_remove.description: Header names to delete from any inherited defaults
headers_remove.title: Headers Remove

transport_type.description: Default transport type inherited by every entry of `transports` that does not declare its own.
transport_type.title: Transport Type

TransportDefaults.title: TransportDefaults

TransportRateLimit.description: Rate limit declaration for a transport. Spec: §Transport Contracts.

time_window_seconds.description: Window length in seconds (int or value-expression)
time_window_seconds.title: Time Window Seconds

TransportRateLimit.title: TransportRateLimit

UrlEncodeDerived.description: `url_encode` — percent-encode a scalar for use inside a URL component.

safe.description: Characters to leave unencoded (default empty string — encode everything)
safe.title: Safe

UrlEncodeDerived.title: UrlEncodeDerived

UrlTemplateDsn.description: Connector-authored URL template DSN with structured bindings.

bindings.description: Map keyed by placeholder name; each binding declares value + encoding.
bindings.title: Bindings

UrlTemplateDsn.title: UrlTemplateDsn

WriteUnit.description:
Preferred write-batch coalescing unit for a destination (issue #87).

Connector-level, not a SQL-only fact: any destination whose write cost is
per-write-operation may declare the batch size it wants the engine's
coalescer to target. At least one of `rows` / `bytes` must be given;
absence of the whole block means "no coalescing preference". Consumed by
the engine batch coalescer (analitiq-engine#384).

bytes.description: Preferred payload size in bytes per write operation (≥ 1).
bytes.title: Bytes

rows.description: Preferred number of rows per write operation (≥ 1).
rows.title: Rows

WriteUnit.title: WriteUnit

description: Public JSON Schema contract for Analitiq connector documents — the authored shape used in source control, PR review, and author-time tooling. `connector_id` is the authored canonical identifier (slug pattern `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_-]*$`). Only `created_at` and `updated_at` are server-managed and absent in the authored shape. Source of truth: analitiq.contracts.connector.Connector (Pydantic).
title: Analitiq Connector
